text:
Ben Carter: quick sync here on deliverability ramp + suppression rules before we flip the switch on onboarding email v2. want to avoid nuking domain rep :grimacing:

Tyler Benson: yep. current plan is a warm/ramp from lifecycle@redwoodinference.com (same sending domain as product updates, but new volume). 

Tyler Benson: proposal:
- Day 1: 10% of eligible cohort (new signups only)
- Day 2: 25%
- Day 3: 50%
- Day 4: 75%
- Day 5: 100%

Tyler Benson: if complaint rate > 0.08% OR hard bounces > 1.5% on any day, we hold at current volume and investigate.

Camila Reyes: +1 on ramp. who’s actually watching the bounce/complaint dashboards daily?

Tyler Benson: I can own. I’m already in SendGrid + Postmaster Tools. I’ll drop a daily note in #marketing and page Ben if we cross thresholds.

Ben Carter: perfect. can you also set up alerts so we don’t rely on humans remembering?

Tyler Benson: yep...

text:
Ben Carter: Dropping subject line variants for onboarding email v2 (Email #1, sent ~30 min after signup). Need a POV on value prop emphasis: latency vs cost vs reliability.

Ben Carter: Current draft options (rough):
A) “Your first LLM request in under 5 minutes”
B) “Ship faster with low-latency LLM inference”
C) “Lower your inference bill (without guesswork)”
D) “Production-grade LLM inference, minus the ops”
E) “Welcome to Redwood — your API key + quickstart”

Ben Carter: Goals: get them to create API key + first successful request. Also don’t want to overpromise.

Soojin Lee: +1 to not overpromising. “under 5 minutes” is attractive but risky. If we keep it, maybe soften: “in minutes” vs “under 5”.

Soojin Lee: Also “low-latency” is strong but can read like benchmark marketing. Reliability angle is safer/true, but may not feel urgent for day 0.

Tyler Benson: Deliverability lens: av...

text:
Clara Moon: Kickoff for a one-page enterprise solution brief + micro-landing. Audience: procurement and platform teams at regulated companies. Must-haves: succinct value prop, three pillars (security controls, SLA guardrails, deployment modes), procurement checklist, lead-routing spec, conservative benchmark callout, CTA A/B plan. Keep it one page max.

Ethan Park: Proposed headline: 'Secure, predictable LLM inference — deploy where your policy requires.' Then three micro-sections: Data Protection, Reliability & SLAs, Deployment Options. Each 2-4 bullets.

Sanjay Rao: For the benchmark hero callout, suggest conservative copy: "Internal Redwood lab: Dedicated + KV caching + 8-bit quantization reduced unit cost ~24% at sustained embedding throughput; p99 generation latency ~100-130ms on our benchmark model. Internal results; customer workloads may vary." Include a tiny chart on the land...

text:
Ava Chen: OK team — kicking off the newsletter + webinar launch sequence for the 'Benchmarking Inference at Scale' webinar. Main goals: drive 2k signups, surface benchmark narrative in post-launch, and move Dev/Platform trial accounts to demo funnel.

Carla Nguyen: quick Q — what's the proposed send cadence? and are we doing a separate launch email vs webinar invite? :thinking_face:

Ava Chen: plan I sketched:
- Day 0: Launch email (all newsletter subscribers segmented) — announce report + webinar CTA
- Day 3: Webinar invite (targeted to active eng/dev cohorts + previous webinar no-shows)
- Day 7: Reminder + social proof (bench highlights)
- Day 14: Follow-up with recording + trial CTA for attendees + nurture for absentees

We will run a short nurture (3 emails) for inbound leads who click but don't sign — 3/7/14 day cadence.

Mike Patel: send windows by region? we should avoid midnig...

title: #marketing: 1749067742
preview:
Soojin Lee: Quick naming q for the benchmark page + PDF. We keep saying “latency/$” internally. Do we want to ship that as the public label? Or something less weird / more legally safe?

Ethan Wallace: +1 it reads a little hacker-y. Also ppl will ask “is lower better?” and “whose $?”

Ben Carter: From growth POV, “l...
text:
Soojin Lee: Quick naming q for the benchmark page + PDF. We keep saying “latency/$” internally. Do we want to ship that as the public label? Or something less weird / more legally safe?

Ethan Wallace: +1 it reads a little hacker-y. Also ppl will ask “is lower better?” and “whose $?”

Ben Carter: From growth POV, “latency per dollar” is super sticky but I worry it’ll get misquoted as “Redwood is cheaper” vs “more cost-efficient for a given latency.”

Nadia Rahman: Technically the metric is `p50_latency_ms / usd` (and also we’ll show p95 separately). Lower is better. It’s basically “milliseconds per USD for a fixed workload definition.”

Henry Cho: Another concern: “latency/$” sounds like a universal truth, but it’s a composite that depends on our pricing + the workload mix. We need the label to signal that.

Jonas Weber: Agree. If we do keep it, we need the tooltip to define it + the ...

text:
Camila Reyes: Week 1 pilot readout (NA cohort) — signal volume is way higher than we expected and it’s starting to feel spammy. Dropping quick stats + proposed fixes.

Camila Reyes: Metrics (Mon–Sun):
- 46 companies got at least 1 routed PLG signal into HubSpot
- 112 tasks created (avg 2.4/company)
- 27 companies had >1 signal family in the same 48h (e.g., “usage spike” + “docs intent” + “429s”)
- 9/112 tasks were marked “not relevant” by AEs in notes (unstructured) + we had 1 explicit “why are you emailing me” complaint come through Support

Camila Reyes: What’s driving noise:
1) Intent signals are too “hair trigger” (single docs view / pricing click)
2) Re-notification is too short (we’re creating new tasks within 48–72h for the same account)
3) Marketing nurture touches are colliding w/ AE outreach (we’re effectively double-pinging)

Tyler Benson: +1. Pulled a quick slice from the ...

title: #marketing: 1746134552
preview:

Tyler Benson: +1. we had a Quartz-ish complaint last week (not the same acct but same vibe...
text:
Camila Reyes: seeing a few early pilot accounts get *both* lifecycle nurture + AE outreach in the same 24h :grimacing: we need suppression rules before this scales. can we align today on what gets suppressed and for how long?

Tyler Benson: +1. we had a Quartz-ish complaint last week (not the same acct but same vibe). in HubSpot we can suppress marketing sends when a company enters a “Sales outreach in progress” state, but we need the rules to be explicit.

Ben Carter: yup. from Growth side, nurture is doing its job (docs/pricing/security views) but when the signal router fires and Sales jumps in, we can’t look uncoordinated. what are we optimizing for? (meeting booked rate + no spam)

Stephanie Nguyen: for the high-intent signals we want AE/SDR touch to win. if marketing hits them with a generic “here’s our dedicated guide” email after I’ve already sent something, it looks automated ...

text:
Ben Carter: Kicking off the regulated workloads Vertical ABM pilot thread. Goal today: lock kickoff agenda, attendee list, and links so we can move fast.

Ben Carter: Proposed kickoff (45 min):
1) Pilot goals + success metrics (10)
2) Vertical choice + ICP hypothesis (10)
3) Asset list + owners + deadlines (15)
4) Target accounts + routing NA/EMEA (5)
5) Weekly cadence + reporting (5)

Ben Carter: Quick question up front: do we start with 1 vertical (finserv) for weeks 1–4 and keep healthcare as optional week 5+ expansion, or try to run both from day 1?

Camila Reyes: +1 to starting with one. We’ll lose time if we don’t get crisp on claims + evidence process.

Soojin Lee: Agree. Finserv is cleaner first pass on “regulated” without triggering HIPAA language landmines in week 1.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: From Security POV: start with finserv. Evidence pack + questionnaire responses are already...

text:
Ava Chen: Quick sync — rolling out a small experiment on the pricing page this week. Goals: clarify tier names, shrink FAQ friction, and try a pared-down cost estimator. Draft plan below.

Jordan Patel: sounds good. are we testing copy + calculator together or separate? want to know how to route webinar signups vs sales leads.

Ava Chen: separate variants — A: new tier names + compact FAQ, B: old names + new estimator. We'll also run C as combo. Analytics will attribute conversions to variant. Webinar CTA will appear only on pages in variants B+C to measure inbound quality.

Priya Rao: For lead routing, please tag leads with `pricing_variant` + `tier_clicked`. Sales ops can route high-intent ("Contact sales" clicks) to AE queue; webinar signups to SDR nurture. I can add the segment to the CRM mapping.

text:
Maya Patel: Quick check-in: we need final hero lines + CTA behavior for Hosted / Dedicated / Private so the benchmarks post and webinar have consistent links and routing. Can we land the copy and hidden UTM rules by Thursday?

Lina Torres: Agreed. Quick principle: Hosted = self-serve, low friction. Dedicated = hand-raise for performance. Private = sales-first with compliance docs.

Ethan Park: Proposed primary CTAs: Hosted = "Start hosted trial"; Dedicated = "Request dedicated demo"; Private = "Contact sales for private deployment". Secondary CTAs: benchmarks, case studies, or pricing.

Noah Kim: Routing specifics: Hosted -> signup flow (utm_source=site&utm_medium=hosted_cta). Dedicated -> /leads/dedicated with hidden `source=dedicated_site` and `estimated_qps` field. Private -> AE queue + auto-send compliance packet. I'll add `page_variant=hero_c` for A/B tracking.

text:
Maya Kapoor: Drafting a one-pager for enterprise buyers (security + SLA + deployment modes). Needs: one-page PDF, compact website block, and webinar. Keep copy tight for procurement teams. Any must-haves?

Ben Carter: Headline idea: 'Secure, SLA-backed LLM inference across Hosted, Dedicated, and Private deployments.' Then 3 short bullets per pillar and one benchmark callout.

Samir Gupta: Routing rules: route to AE if compliance_needed == true OR annual_commit >= 50k. Otherwise self-serve with sales opt-in. Add fields: deployment_preference, compliance_needed, expected_token_volume. Example lead JSON: `\{\"deployment_preference\":\"Hosted|Dedicated|Private\",\"compliance_needed\":true,\"expected_token_volume\":\"number\"\}`

Lena Fox: Legal check: wording must be careful. Say "supports customer-managed KMS, VPC/private control plane, and audit logs." For SLAs use "SLA-backed uptime su...

text:
Ava Chen: Quick sync on benchmark webinar + launch playbook. Focus: unit economics (tokens -> $), latency tradeoffs, and routing. Need 3 simple charts, two messaging tracks (startup vs enterprise), and a clear inbound lead flow. Can we sketch a minimal deck outline?

Jonah Patel: Love it. My three-chart shortlist: 1) Cost per 1M tokens by model family (bars, call out batching/quantization assumptions), 2) p95 latency comparison by deployment mode (hosted / dedicated / private) as small multiples, 3) Throughput vs cost curve: tokens/sec at target p95 SLO with fallback strategies annotated.

Samira Rao: For chart 1 let's use model buckets: small (<2B), medium (2-20B), large (20-70B), xlarge (70B+). Show median cost and a light error band. Callout under chart: "assumes 8x batching + int8 quantization where supported".

text:
Ava Chen: Quick thread — prep for next month's webinar + benchmark narrative. Goal: make the unit-economics story front-and-center (tokens -> $) while still showing the latency/throughput tradeoffs. Need 3 chart hypotheses, short positioning bullets for startups vs enterprise, and a concrete inbound routing plan for leads. Can we sketch this out?

Jonah Patel: +1. Three-chart idea I like: 1) cost-per-1M tokens vs model family (bars, normalized), 2) latency CDF by deployment mode (hosted/dedicated/private) for 10k prompt distribution, 3) throughput curves showing tokens/sec at target p95 SLOs. Short and scannable.

Samira Rao: For chart 1, propose buckets: <2B, 2–20B, 20–70B, 70B+. Use median quantized costs (with error bars). Callout: quantization & batching assumptions under the bars.
